items.back: When CA or MCI alert displays: evaluate reason without delay and take appropriate action. If another controller involved: initiate coordination (not required when immediate action dictated). Suppress/Inhibit available per criteria.

E-MSAW = En Route Minimum Safe Altitude Warning. Indefinite alert suppression: for aircraft known to fly at altitudes that activate E-MSAW in one or more MIA areas (typically military low-level routes). Computer entry of suppression = acknowledgment that action has/will be taken.

Altitude field must always reflect current aircraft status. Do not modify assigned/interim altitude for aircraft outside your jurisdiction without verbal coordination. When cleared to different altitude: enter new assigned, interim, procedure (VNAV SID/STAR), or LIA as appropriate. Route data: do not modify outside jurisdiction without verbal coordination/LOA.

Required when Mode C unavailable or unreliable. Enter: (a) when aircraft reaches assigned altitude, (b) when issued climb/descent clearance, (c) minimum each 10,000 ft during climb to/descent from FL180 and above.

items.front: Altitude limits for Mode C target display (5-13-5)
items.back: For Merging Target Procedures, display Mode C targets and limited data blocks covering sector's altitude stratum PLUS: 1,200 ft above highest/below lowest where 1,000 ft vertical separation applies; 2,200 ft above highest/below lowest where 2,000 ft vertical separation applies.
May override sector eligibility only when: (a) prior coordination is effected, OR (b) flight is within the control jurisdiction of the sector.
Do NOT use coast tracks in the application of either radar or nonradar separation criteria.

Initiate coast tracks only in FLAT (Flight Plan Aided Tracking) mode. Exception: 'free' coast tracking as reminder for aircraft without flight plan info. Before coast track: ensure departure message/progress report for current position entered. Asap after radar surveillance: initiate radar tracking.

When holding is ATC-initiated and EFC is other than 'no delay expected': (a) enter hold message, (b) maintain paired track, (c) enter EFC time, (d) enter non-published holding instructions. Verbally coordinate non-published holding when handing off in hold. EFC entry constitutes coordination between ERAM sectors.
ERAM provides a visual indicator of Special Activity Airspace (SAA) status on the controller display.
